title: Soy wax jar candle with citrus
subtitle: A 200 ml container candle with sweet orange and lemon essential oils
excerpt: A container soy wax candle scented with sweet orange and lemon essential oils at a combined 7% fragrance load. Citrus essential oils are lighter than florals and give a fresher cold throw; the warm scent in the burn is bright and clean without the sweetness of synthetic citrus fragrance oils.
sourceNotes: CandleScience soy wax fragrance notes (candlescience.com, May 2026), citrus EO performance in soy wax. Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014), pp. 342-346, phototoxicity note for citrus EOs in leave-on cosmetics (not relevant for candle burn products).

content.text: A soy wax container candle with sweet orange and lemon essential oils. The citrus blend at 7% of wax weight gives a clean, fresh scent. Sweet orange (Citrus sinensis) provides the round, warm citrus note; lemon (Citrus limon) adds a sharper, brighter top note. Combined in a 4:3 ratio (sweet orange dominant), the blend reads as a full citrus rather than a single fruit.

Citrus essential oils are among the more volatile fragrance materials and have a shorter scent life in a candle than heavier florals or woods. Allow at least 1 week of cure before the first burn to let the fragrance bind to the wax fully.
Melt wax on a simmering-water bath, not directly on the hob. Never leave melting wax unattended. Keep a kitchen fire blanket within reach.

items.name: Sweet orange essential oil
items.prepNote: cold-pressed sweet orange (Citrus sinensis) essential oil. 4% of wax weight (6 g ≈ 6 ml).

items.name: Lemon essential oil
items.prepNote: cold-pressed lemon (Citrus limon) essential oil. 3% of wax weight (approximately 5 g ≈ 5 ml).

items.name: Candle wick, pre-tabbed (CD-12)
items.prepNote: pre-tabbed cotton wick sized for a 7 cm diameter jar.

content.text: Adhere the wick tab to the centre base of the jar. Thread the wick through the centring bar and lay it across the top of the jar so the wick is vertical and centred. Leave 1-2 cm extending above the bar.
Melt the wax, add fragrance, pour

Place the soy wax in the pouring pitcher in a saucepan of simmering water. Stir until fully melted. Remove the pitcher from the water bath and allow the wax to cool. When the probe reads 55°C, add the sweet orange and lemon essential oils and stir for 2 minutes to incorporate. When the wax cools to 60-65°C, pour steadily into the jar. Do not move for 24 hours.
Trim the wick to 5 mm. Allow the first burn to run for 3-4 hours until the melt pool reaches the full jar diameter. This is the
that prevents tunnelling on subsequent burns.

Effectively indefinite unlit, stored away from direct sunlight. Burn within 12-18 months for the best scent; citrus essential oils are among the most volatile fragrance materials and fade faster than other fragrance families in candles. The wax and wick remain functional indefinitely.
